title: Was scrolling on fxbook systems and found this. Is this even real ?
author: Expensive-Fun-1014

comments.author: Obelisk_810
comments.body: If you have a doubt there is no doubt

comments.author: Former-Pomelo3355
comments.body:
The longer I’ve been in trading, the more I learn about shady things brokers will do with their clients that promote their IB’s the best. 

Some brokers will give you a “real” account with fake money. They can populate any trades and any withdrawals. To be clear, I’m NOT saying that’s what this is, but your skepticism is healthy and not misplaced. It could be real, impossible to tell without getting more provable context.

Most legit EA sellers have no problem giving you an investor PW for a week to watch it live trade. Even if you’re copy trading, unethically, you can only do it for a week before you have to pay for it.

replies.author: Expensive-Fun-1014
replies.body: This is the same idea i had in my mind. I mean logically, if you have an ea that can turn ur 100$ to 80k$ in one month then dude wtf is the purpose of showing it off on fxbook ? Just go and live ur best life being a millionaire.

comments.author: PenCreative3945
comments.body: Very suspicious, some facts to check: the broker reputation, only one month of trades, history of trades hidden in Myfxbook...

comments.author: ronnooi
comments.body:
If you’re thinking of contacting the owner to use this system… honestly, don’t waste your time.

I was once in your exact position. I saw the results on Myfxbook and thought, “Wow, this system is insane.” So I reached out to the owner, wanting access to that gold scalping system.

That’s when the sales pitch started.

He created urgency and pushed a membership package for $500, saying you’ll get access to all his systems — including a chance to use the gold scalping strategy. Out of curiosity (and honestly, a bit of FOMO), I paid.

Only after paying, I realised the truth.

You don’t actually get access to the gold scalping system. You’re only given a few other systems — and even those didn’t perform well. To even stand a chance of using the gold system, you’re required to deposit funds into their affiliate broker.

And then?

You’re entered into a “lucky draw” that happens maybe twice a month… where only a handful of users (like 2–3 people out of hundreds) get access.

Think about that.

You’re basically paying, depositing more money, and still relying on luck just to try the system.

I used the systems they provided within the membership available products for about a month — and I was losing money.

Looking back, it’s pretty clear what’s happening:
They use the results of that gold system as a marketing hook to attract people, create hype, and get you to pay. But once you’re inside, the reality is completely different.

So if you’re considering joining, just know this:
What you see is not what you get.

Don’t fall into the same trap I did.

comments.author: mkejross
comments.body:
It’s likely not fake. Some of you have such closed minds. I’ve personally turned 100 into 10k on 2 occasions trading gold. So i cant image taking it to 80k would be impossible.

The broker is a regulated broker, so he’s not asked them to fake his trading history 😂
